summary: Successfully verified PDF invoice generation functionality. Centralized PDF utility at /app/frontend/src/utils/invoicePdfGenerator.js is working correctly. Both Sales History 'Download PDF' and POS receipt 'PDF' buttons generate A4 invoices with proper formatting including MRP column and CGST/SGST split.
passed_tests: POS Page loads and displays products - SUCCESS
Quick Cash Checkout modal shows correct total - SUCCESS
Full Checkout flow completes sale - SUCCESS
POS Receipt modal shows CGST (2.5%) and SGST (2.5%) split correctly - SUCCESS
POS Receipt PDF button click - SUCCESS (Toast: 'Invoice PDF downloaded!')
Sales History page loads with 12+ sales records - SUCCESS
Sales History action menu shows Download PDF option - SUCCESS
Sales History Download PDF click - SUCCESS (Toast: 'Invoice INV-000012 downloaded')
No JavaScript console errors related to PDF generation - SUCCESS

sales_history_pdf_download.description: Download PDF button in action menu generates A4 invoice

pos_receipt_pdf_download.description: PDF button in receipt modal generates A4 invoice

gst_split_display.description: Receipt shows CGST (2.5%) ₹35.71 and SGST (2.5%) ₹35.71 for 5% GST

critical_code_review_comments: invoicePdfGenerator.js properly exports generateProfessionalInvoicePDF and downloadInvoicePDF functions
SalesPage.js handleDownloadPDF correctly requires and calls downloadInvoicePDF from centralized utility
POSPage.js handleDownloadPDF correctly requires and calls downloadInvoicePDF from centralized utility
PDF includes MRP column in items table (line 125 in invoicePdfGenerator.js)
CGST/SGST split calculation is correct (gstRate / 2 for each)
seed_data_creation: Created 2 test sales (INV-000013, INV-000014) during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: PDF invoice generation is fully functional. The centralized utility at /app/frontend/src/utils/invoicePdfGenerator.js handles PDF generation for both SalesPage and POSPage. Sales History has 14+ sales records. Today's Sales shows ₹1,500.00 with 1 order after testing.
console_errors_observed: GeolocationPositionError - Unrelated to PDF functionality, occurs due to location services

pdf_features_confirmed.styling: Red primary color (#C81E1E), proper alignment, professional layout
